title: Shingles - aftercare
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Shingles aftercare includes keeping the rash clean, avoiding scratching, and using clinician-directed medicines.
Patients should seek care for rash near the eye, worsening symptoms, fever, confusion, severe headache, weakness, or other concerning symptoms.
Avoid exposing people at high risk for chickenpox complications until lesions are crusted.
Pain can persist after the rash and should be followed with a clinician.
